rent.note: Monthly snapshot, not live counts — every listings site's terms prohibit automated access. ZORI updates monthly; HUD FMR annually.

stats.label: Typical asking rent, Burlington metro (+1.6% in a year)

managers.name: Redstone
managers.what: The area's largest manager — market-rate buildings across Burlington, South Burlington, Winooski and Colchester, plus lease-by-bed student housing (Redstone Lofts, Redstone Commons, Spinner Place).
managers.notes: Student buildings lease many months before fall move-in; their UVM-affiliated buildings are restricted to UVM students and staff.

managers.name: Bissonette Properties
managers.what: Big family-owned local landlord — apartments and houses in Burlington (Old North End, UVM area), Winooski, and most surrounding towns.
managers.notes: Real searchable availability page with city/beds/pets filters. Office at 100 North St, weekdays 9–5.

managers.name: S.D. Ireland Properties
managers.what: Newer, amenity-heavy market-rate buildings — Bank Street Apartments and One25 Cambrian Way in Burlington, Bayberry Commons, Severance Corners.
managers.notes: All buildings pet-friendly; weekend showings by appointment.

managers.name: Offenhartz Management
managers.what: Local landlord since 1989 with 100+ units — studios to 3-beds in Burlington, South Burlington and Winooski, roughly $1,400–$2,800.
managers.notes: Office at 196 Battery St; their available-units page stays genuinely current.

managers.name: Rieley Properties (Burlington Apartments)
managers.what: One of Burlington's largest landlords — student housing near UVM and Champlain College, downtown apartments, plus non-student units at Finney Crossing in Williston.
managers.notes: Texting welcomed; rated a top-10 landlord in UVM student surveys. Student units follow the June–May cycle — tour in fall for next year.

managers.name: O'Brien Brothers
managers.what: Family developer-manager in South Burlington — market-rate and subsidized apartments across the greater Burlington area.
managers.notes: Portfolio spans affordable to market-rate; 24/7 on-call maintenance.

managers.name: Larkin Realty
managers.what: Family developer-owner since 1980 — apartment complexes along the Shelburne Road corridor in South Burlington.
managers.notes: Availability page sometimes shows zero — inventory turns over fast, so emailing directly is exactly the right move here.

managers.name: Handy's Property Rentals
managers.what: Handy family portfolio of roughly 250 units — older apartments downtown and near UVM and Champlain, many pet-friendly with parking.
managers.notes: No listings page of their own — they post to Zillow — which makes a direct email even more valuable. Office at 75 S. Winooski Ave.

managers.name: Gabe Handy Rentals
managers.what: Separate Handy-family operation run from Essex — apartments across Chittenden and Franklin counties.
managers.notes: Rental agent line: (802) 404-1333. Apply through their own site, not the listing platforms.

managers.name: Full Circle Property Management
managers.what: Third-party manager for many individual owners around Chittenden County — took over the longtime Coburn & Feeley portfolio in 2019.
managers.notes: Listings page reflects live availability. If you rented from Coburn & Feeley back in the day, this is where that portfolio went.

managers.name: Champlain Housing Trust
managers.what: Nonprofit community land trust — the region's biggest source of permanently affordable rentals; most units via waitlist.
managers.notes: Their listings page splits Available vs. Waitlist — waits can run 10+ months but some properties turn fast. Income limits apply. 88 King St.

managers.name: Burlington Housing Authority
managers.what: Public housing authority — subsidized apartments and Section 8 vouchers in Burlington; waitlist-driven.
managers.notes: Expect a couple of weeks for a response on vouchers or waitlist placement — start early. 65 Main St, Suite 101.

managers.name: Winooski Housing Authority
managers.what: Winooski's public housing authority — affordable and senior housing in the city of Winooski.
managers.notes: No listings page — apply through the Applicants page and join waitlists. Phones staffed Mon–Thu 8:30–4, Friday mornings.

managers.name: Summit Properties
managers.what: Manages ~1,400 apartments, mostly affordable plus some market-rate — locally: Packard Lofts, Brandywine Lofts, Parc Terrace and Great Cedars.
managers.notes: No central availability search — each property page lists its own leasing contact, and income limits apply at many buildings.

managers.name: Cathedral Square
managers.what: Nonprofit senior housing — 28 communities and ~1,100 homes for older adults and people with disabilities across three counties.
managers.notes: Waitlists run long — nearly 1,400 households at last count — so apply well before you need it.

managers.name: HallKeen Management (Keen's Crossing)
managers.what: National manager whose local footprint is Keen's Crossing in Winooski — 213 riverfront units mixing market-rate and income-restricted, heat included.
managers.notes: About half the units are income-restricted — ask which pool you're applying into.

managers.name: Five Seasons Property Management
managers.what: Boutique manager since 2008 — single-family homes, condos and small multifamily throughout Chittenden County, plus furnished mid-term rentals.
managers.notes: The one to email if you want a house or condo rather than a big building. 19 Marble Ave, Burlington.

managers.name: Wheeler Property Management
managers.what: Old-school manager since 1986 — small apartment buildings, duplexes and houses across greater Burlington.
managers.notes: No listings on their own site — everything current goes to Craigslist, so search Burlington Craigslist for “Wheeler” or just email.

managers.name: Strong Will Property Management
managers.what: Small local manager of 100+ single-family and multifamily units across greater Burlington, based in South Burlington.
managers.notes: Online rent payment and maintenance portal. 68 Randall St, South Burlington.

managers.name: Nest VT
managers.what: South End-based leasing and management shop — houses and apartments around Chittenden County, generally mid-to-upper market.
managers.notes: Recent listings ran roughly $2,500–$3,750/mo. Office hours weekdays 8–5.

managers.name: JT's Real Estate Management
managers.what: Downtown Burlington apartments aimed at students and young professionals, 1–5 bedrooms, plus short-term and sublet options.
managers.notes: The phone spells 802-552-RENT. Lists sublets too — useful if you need something mid-year.

managers.name: BTV Apartments
managers.what: Small local landlord with downtown, Old North End and South End apartments (Pearl St, Loomis St, Decatur St), studio through 5-bed.
managers.notes: Leases follow the June 1 cycle; they've offered 10-month leases for late-summer move-ins.

managers.name: Burlington Vermont Apartments
managers.what: Small landlord with studio-to-7-bedroom apartments and houses near UVM, Champlain College and downtown.
managers.notes: Email-only — no phone published. Inventory skews to student-cycle group houses.

managers.name: Nedde Real Estate
managers.what: Mostly commercial, but owns two newer downtown residential buildings on Pine Street — 77 Residences and The Nest, 49 apartments each.
managers.notes: Their listings page mixes commercial and residential — look for the 77 Residences and The Nest entries.

sources.name: Zillow Rentals
sources.good_for: The biggest single pool of professionally listed Burlington rentals, with the best filters and map search.
sources.gotcha: Pushes Zillow's own $35 application flow that some landlords never look at; duplicate listings inflate the counts.

sources.name: Apartments.com
sources.good_for: Best for the larger managed buildings and complexes — verified listings, 3D tours, and a for-rent-by-owner section.
sources.gotcha: The headline count sweeps in surrounding towns and counts units, not buildings; small local landlords barely show up here.

sources.name: Craigslist
sources.good_for: Where small local landlords still post — often the cheapest units, and ones that never hit Zillow.
sources.gotcha: Now a statewide Vermont search, so filter by map. Classic scam territory: never wire money or pay a deposit before seeing the unit in person.

sources.name: Facebook Marketplace
sources.good_for: Fast-moving individual-landlord and sublet listings — good for catching units hours after they post.
sources.gotcha: Requires a Facebook login, and it has the highest scam density of any source here — fake landlords clone real listings, so verify ownership before sending anything.

sources.name: FB Group: Burlington, Vermont Area Rentals
sources.good_for: The big public rentals group — landlords and tenants post directly, including apartments that never get listed anywhere else.
sources.gotcha: A firehose of sublets, ISO posts, and the occasional scam. Same rule: no money before an in-person showing.

sources.name: FB Group: UVM Housing, Room, Rental & Sublet
sources.good_for: The student-market group — sublets, lease takeovers, and roommate openings near campus, busiest May through August.
sources.gotcha: Student-cycle timing: June 1 leases get signed six to nine months out, and many posts are rooms, not whole apartments.

sources.name: Front Porch Forum
sources.good_for: Neighbor-to-neighbor listings with real names attached — the highest-trust source in town, where quiet listings surface first.
sources.gotcha: You have to sign up for your neighborhood's forum and read the email digests — there's no browsing it from outside.

sources.name: UVM Off-Campus Housing
sources.good_for: UVM's official off-campus listing database — vetted landlord listings for the student and staff market, plus roommate matching.
sources.gotcha: Full search requires a UVM email; everyone else only sees the publicly indexed listing pages.

sources.name: Seven Days Classifieds
sources.good_for: The alt-weekly's classifieds — genuinely local, older-school landlords who don't use Zillow, plus sublet and housemate categories.
sources.gotcha: Thin inventory next to the national sites, statewide by default, and listings can sit stale.

sources.name: PadMapper
sources.good_for: Map-first aggregator — handy for visually scanning everything at once.
sources.gotcha: Almost pure aggregation, so listings lag and some are already rented; nothing here is exclusive.
